joke.raw_joke: A woman tells her husband, "the clock fell off the wall and almost hit my mother in the head!" The husband replie - 'Shitty clock, always late!'

blocks.content:
MILO_OFFICIAL_SCRIPT_v1

seed_id: seed-031
edit_level: rewrite
script:
  setup: "My wife said the clock fell off the wall and almost hit her mother."
  punchline: "Shitty clock. Always late."
  tag: ~
changes_made: Converted third-person framing to first-person for VO delivery. Cut "in the head" — redundant after "almost hit." Kept profanity — "shitty" is doing real work here, it sells the disappointment. Clean version ("that clock") loses the edge.
caption_notes: Setup as one card. Punchline as second card — "Shitty clock." and "Always late." can be one card or split into two beats depending on Iris concept. Single card preferred.
timing_notes: Setup delivered straight, casual, like telling a friend. 0.5s pause before punchline. Punchline deadpan — the humor is in the flat disappointment, not the anger. Emphasize "always."
tts_notes: ~
quality_flag: strong

RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1

):

1. Jerry Gold — Husband / VO narrator
   persona: Deadpan News Host
   why: The punchline is a single flat verdict. Jerry does not react. He evaluates. "Shitty clock. Always late." is not frustration — it is a ruling. Nobody delivers rulings like Jerry.
   notes: VO preferred. If on-camera: no expression, possible slow shrug. Not looking at his wife when he speaks.

2. Zoe Sparks — Wife (setup carrier)
   persona: Optimistic Teacher
   why: Zoe reads as genuinely alarmed, not performatively alarmed. The joke requires a real emotional setup so Jerry can flatten it. Her wide-eyed mid-report energy is exactly right.
   notes: Casual home wardrobe. No props. She is telling a story, not staging one.

3. Tasha Trend — Mother-in-law (near-miss victim, background)
   persona: Council Candidate (softened to Visiting Guest)
   why: Tasha carries inherent self-importance. She arrives with an agenda. The idea that a clock nearly hit her is funnier because she clearly feels the universe owes her better. Visually neutral relative to the joke target role — no uncomfortable racial subtext. Strong background anchor.
   notes: Remove any activist/campaign props. Upscale casual: blazer or structured cardigan, minimal accessories. Seated or standing near the wall, unaware of how close it was. Her baseline expression should read as vaguely dissatisfied with the visit so far — that is Tasha in a home environment without a podium.

Shot Intent:
- Setup: Clock-gap on wall visible. Zoe in foreground, mid-report, visibly distressed. Tasha in soft background — touching hair or adjusting collar where the clock almost hit, still slightly indignant about it.
- Punchline: Hold on Jerry. Full deadpan. Half-beat pause. "Always late." — if splitting to two caption beats, cut on that word. If single card, let the pause do the work.

Caption alignment (per Milo v1):
- Setup card: "My wife said the clock fell off the wall and almost hit her mother."
- Punchline card: "Shitty clock. Always late." (single card preferred)

Status: CAST LOCKED — ready for Iris visual concept.

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v1

seed_id: seed-031
concepts_count: 3
surreal_flagged: false (but Concept C goes surreal voluntarily — this joke earns it)

## Concept A — "The Living Room Report"
Style: Domestic sitcom, warm lighting, classic setup-punchline framing
Duration: 8.8s
Scenes: 4 (empty wall hook → Zoe report w/ Tasha bg → Jerry pause → Jerry punchline deadpan)
Jerry hook: Clean, safe, classic two-hander. Default greenlight — nothing gets in the way of the punchline.
Vince: 9:16, 8.8s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[punchline, setup+Tasha]

## Concept B — "The Newsdesk Verdict"
Style: Deadpan news broadcast, Jerry as anchor
Duration: 9.4s
Scenes: 5 (breaking news chyron → anchor setup → editorial pause → punchline as commentary → "back to you" tag)
Jerry hook: Jerry gets the whole show. Deadpan News Host is his strongest persona. Format does the heavy lifting.
Vince: 9:16, 9.4s, 5 scenes, mustKeep=[punchline, anchor delivery]

## Concept C [SURREAL] — "The Clock's Confession"
Style: Courtroom drama meets absurdist animation — the clock is on trial
Duration: 10.2s
surreal_element: Anthropomorphic clock as defendant, Jerry as judge. Clock literally arrives late (shows 3:15 vs courtroom's 3:00). "Always late" becomes a judicial ruling.
Weirdness budget: 6/10 (Pixar-meets-Judge-Judy)
Scenes: 5 (courtroom reveal + nervous clock → Zoe prosecutes → transformation beat (time mismatch reveal) → gavel + punchline → Tasha vindication nod)
Jerry hook: Weird on purpose. AI will make it weirder. That's the bit. Courtroom sells "always late" as a legal finding.
Vince: 9:16, 10.2s, 5 scenes, mustKeep=[gavel+punchline, transformation beat, clock reveal]

Full scene-by-scene breakdowns, master prompts, caption plans, voice timing, and fallbacks included in complete notes.

quality_gate: PASS (3 distinct concepts, all timestamped, all feasible, punchline clear, vertical preserved, fallbacks provided, Jerry hooks present)
status: ready_for_jerry_review

JERRY_GREENLIGHT_v1 ---
JERRY_GREENLIGHT_v1

seed_id: seed-031
selected_concept: B
decision: GREENLIGHT
confidence: High
rationale: Concept B puts Jerry in his strongest operating mode — the deadpan anchor delivering an editorial verdict. The news format reframes "Always late" as institutional judgment rather than a personal reaction, which is funnier and cleaner. Concept A is reliable but domestic two-handers are our default register. Concept C has a premise worth admiring and then declining — the anthropomorphic clock plus transformation beat plus courtroom scene is three failure points for a 9-second joke. Not this week.
cost_note: 5 scenes, single environment (news desk), no complex props or transitions. One of the cheaper builds at this runtime.
must_keep: breaking-news chyron hook (0–2s), anchor delivery of punchline with half-beat pause before "Always late"
trim_if_needed: tag at 8.8–9.4s — the video ends clean without it; the punchline is the exit
next_owner: Vince

---

JERRY_MONEY_BRIEF_v1 ---
JERRY_MONEY_BRIEF_v1

seed_id: seed-031
selected_concept: B — "The Newsdesk Verdict"
objective: Jerry delivers a deadpan news broadcast verdict on a domestic clock incident — the joke lands when "Always late" reads as judicial rather than personal.

format:
  aspect_ratio: 9:16
  target_duration_sec: 9.4
  fps: 24
  resolution_target: 1080x1920

cast_and_personas:
  - Jerry Gold | Deadpan News Host | on-camera anchor, full frame
  - Zoe Sparks | referenced only — Jerry quotes her report, does not appear
  - Tasha Trend | referenced only — the subject of the incident, does not appear

final_script_lines:
  1. [CHYRON] "BREAKING: DOMESTIC CLOCK INCIDENT"
  2. [JERRY VO/ON-CAM] "My wife said the clock fell off the wall and almost hit her mother."
  3. [PAUSE — editorial beat, silence]
  4. [JERRY] "Shitty clock."
  5. [JERRY] "Always late."
  6. [OPTIONAL TAG] Jerry turns back to camera. Small nod. Freeze or end card.

scene_timeline:
  0.0–1.8s: Breaking news chyron full-frame or lower-third. Jerry visible at anchor desk. Serious expression. Studio lighting.
  1.8–4.5s: Jerry delivers setup line. Steady medium shot. No movement. Not smiling.
  4.5–6.0s: Silence. Jerry glances slightly off-camera. Returns to frame. The pause carries the joke.
  6.0–7.8s: "Shitty clock." Flat. Looking directly at camera.
  7.8–8.8s: "Always late." Same register. No change in expression.
  8.8–9.4s: [Optional] Small nod. Freeze or cut to black.

camera_and_motion_notes:
  - Static medium shot throughout. No pans, no zooms, no rack focus.
  - Jerry centered in 9:16 frame, head and chest visible.
  - News desk in lower third. Studio backdrop: neutral dark blue or charcoal.
  - DO NOT cut away during the punchline. Hold on Jerry.
  - If two-shot variation is attempted, keep Zoe/Tasha out — they are referenced, not visible.

caption_plan:
  1.8–4.5s: "My wife said the clock fell off the wall and almost hit her mother."
  6.0–8.8s: "Shitty clock. Always late."
  Note: Single punchline card preferred. If split, break after "Shitty clock." at 7.8s.

audio_voice_notes:
  - Jerry Gold: Deadpan delivery, near-zero affect. Setup is flat — this is a news report, not a complaint. Punchline is quieter than the setup, not louder. Emphasize "always" — slightly drawn out, as if reading from official record. No laugh, no smirk.

must_keep:
  - Breaking news chyron in first 2 seconds
  - Full pause beat (4.5–6.0s) — do not compress or cut
  - Punchline on static shot with no motion

optional_trims:
  - Tag at 8.8–9.4s (small nod / freeze) — cut if runtime is tight
  - Chyron text can be simplified if rendering is slow

cost_guardrails:
  max_generation_attempts: 3
  max_fallback_attempts: 2
  stop_conditions:
    - If anchor framing loses Jerry face in any scene: fallback to Concept A (domestic two-hander, 4 scenes)
    - If punchline scene fails twice: hold on a still frame + caption only
    - Do NOT attempt Concept C as fallback — too complex

deliverable_checklist:
  - [ ] Scene 1: Chyron + Jerry framed at anchor desk
  - [ ] Scene 2: Setup delivery, static, no motion
  - [ ] Scene 3: Pause beat, Jerry off-camera glance
  - [ ] Scene 4: Punchline — "Shitty clock."
  - [ ] Scene 5: "Always late."
  - [ ] Captions synced and readable
  - [ ] No scenes exceed 9:16 composition
  - [ ] No brand safety flags

handoff_to: Vince
